2603.26248.title: Automatic Speech Recognition for Documenting Endangered Languages: Case Study of Ikema Miyakoan
2603.26248.paper_summary: Language endangerment poses a major challenge to linguistic diversity worldwide, and technological advances have opened new avenues for documentation and revitalization. Among these, automatic speech recognition (ASR) has shown increasing potential to assist in the transcription of endangered language data. This study focuses on Ikema, a severely endangered Ryukyuan language spoken in Okinawa, Japan, with approximately 1,300 remaining speakers, most of whom are over 60 years old. We present an ongoing effort to develop an ASR system for Ikema based on field recordings. Specifically, we (1) construct a {\totaldatasethours}-hour speech corpus from field recordings, (2) train an ASR model that achieves a character error rate as low as 15\%, and (3) evaluate the impact of ASR assistance on the efficiency of speech transcription. Our results demonstrate that ASR integration can substantially reduce transcription time and cognitive load, offering a practical pathway toward scalable, technology-supported documentation of endangered languages.
2603.26248.paper_summary_zh: 語言瀕危對全球語言多樣性構成了重大挑戰，而技術進步則為文獻記錄和復興開啟了新的途徑。在這些技術中，自動語音識別（ASR）顯示出越來越大的潛力，能夠協助瀕危語言數據的轉錄。本研究聚焦於Ikema，這是一種在日本沖繩使用的嚴重瀕危琉球語，現存約有1,300名說話者，其中大多數年齡超過60歲。我們展示了一項正在進行的努力，旨在基於實地錄音為Ikema開發一個ASR系統。具體而言，我們（1）從實地錄音中構建了一個{\totaldatasethours}小時的語音語料庫，（2）訓練了一個ASR模型，其字符錯誤率低至15\%，以及（3）評估ASR輔助對語音轉錄效率的影響。我們的結果顯示，ASR整合可以顯著減少轉錄時間和認知負擔，為可擴展的、技術支持的瀕危語言文獻記錄提供了一條實際途徑。
2603.26248.author: Chihiro Taguchi et.al.
2603.26248.authors: Chihiro Taguchi, Yukinori Takubo, David Chiang
